heading: ## Pre-registration plan
body: The study will be pre-registered on the Open Science Framework (OSF) prior to data collection. The registration will include the primary hypotheses, experimental design, and analysis plan. The OSF ID placeholder is `osf.io/TBD`. The planned pre-registration date is within two weeks of grant award (TBD).

heading: ## Sample size calculation
body: For each falsification test (FT1.1, FT1.2, FT2.1), a sample size calculation will be performed to ensure adequate statistical power. The general formula for a two-sample comparison is: n = (1.96 + 0.84)² · σ² / δ², where 1.96 is the z-score for α=0.05 (two-tailed), 0.84 is the z-score for power=0.80, σ² is the expected variance, and δ is the minimum detectable effect size. Specific parameters (effect size, variance, α, power) will be determined based on pilot data or literature and will be documented in the pre-registration. Placeholder: N = TBD.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Risk | Probability (0-1) | Impact (1-5) | Mitigation plan |
|------|-------------------|--------------|-----------------|
| Low reproducibility of C1 in HSC | 0.4 | 5 | Use multiple HSC isolation protocols; validate with orthogonal methods (e.g., super-resolution microscopy). |
| Negative result for P6 (relapse prediction) | 0.3 | 4 | Pre-register analysis plan; include positive controls; plan for Bayesian re-analysis if null. |
| Key personnel turnover | 0.2 | 3 | Cross-train lab members; maintain detailed protocols; have backup collaborators. |
| Budget overrun | 0.3 | 2 | Monthly budget reviews; contingency fund (10% of total); prioritize critical experiments. |
| Data management failure | 0.1 | 4 | Use version-controlled repositories (Git); regular backups; data management plan in place. |

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:
The following potential partners have been identified for collaboration:
- **Dr. X (Yale University)** — live-cell imaging and centriole dynamics.
- **Dr. Y (Stanford University)** — HSC transplantation and in vivo lineage tracing.
- **Dr. Z (Max Planck Institute)** — proteomics and PTM analysis.
- **Dr. W (University of Cambridge)** — computational modeling and statistical analysis.
Formal agreements and letters of support will be obtained prior to grant submission.
